content.text:
<user_query>
After the latest tier_guard and tier_resolver updates, confirm whether any HIGH parity issues remain in this access-control surface: backend/middleware/tier_guard.py, backend/tier_resolver.py, backend/routes/deps.py, backend/auth_supabase.py, backend/routes/email.py, frontend/src/App.js, frontend/src/lib/tierResolver.js, frontend/src/config/routeAccessConfig.js, frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.js, frontend/src/components/DashboardLayout.js, frontend/src/config/tiers.js, frontend/src/config/pricingTiers.js. Return HIGH findings only or NONE.
</user_query>
Reading the listed access-control files to compare backend and frontend tier/route logic and flag HIGH-severity parity gaps.
Reading `LaunchRoute` in `App.js` and the waitlist section of `DashboardLayout.js`.
Checking how `DashboardLayout` defines `isSA` and whether `super_admin` role (non-master) is handled consistently.
Checking for API path patterns that could bypass waitlist while the UI blocks, and verifying `LaunchRoute` vs `checkRouteAccess` alignment.

## HIGH

**Centralized API tier/waitlist enforcement never runs.** `TierGuardMiddleware` in `backend/middleware/tier_guard.py` is the **only** caller of `check_api_access` and `check_route_access` in `backend/tier_resolver.py` (no other references in the repo). That middleware is **not** added in `configure_middleware` (only `NoCacheAPIMiddleware`, `RateLimitAPIMiddleware`, CORS, and `SessionMiddleware` are registered), and nothing sets `request.state.user`, so even the middleware’s own gate would no-op if it were mounted. So the maps and logic in `tier_resolver.py` that are meant to mirror frontend routing/waitlist policy are **not applied** on incoming `/api/*` requests, while `frontend/src/App.js` (`LaunchRoute`), `frontend/src/config/routeAccessConfig.js`, and `frontend/src/lib/tierResolver.js` **do** enforce those rules in the UI. That is a **HIGH** backend–frontend parity gap on this access-control surface (UI gated, blanket API checks from these modules inactive).

---

**Within the listed files only, for static alignment:** `WAITLIST_ROUTES` / `WAITLIST_API_PREFIXES` line up with `routeAccessConfig` waitlist routes; `resolveTier` / legacy paid mapping matches `tier_resolver.resolve_tier`; `LaunchRoute` paid vs waitlist rules match the intent of `checkRouteAccess`; `auth_supabase` / `deps` / `email` usage of `resolve_tier` is consistent with the same tier model; `tiers.js` / `pricingTiers.js` stay on the free + starter (+ display admin) model. Those do not surface additional **HIGH** parity defects beyond the inactive tier guard path above.
